findings.finding_title: Mobile Add-to-Cart Is Below the First Viewport
findings.finding_body: On mobile, the first viewport shows the promo bar, header, search, product image, thumbnails, title, color selector, and price, but the purchase button is not visible until the next scroll position. The button sits around 902 CSS px from the top on an 844 CSS px viewport.
findings.observation: On mobile, the first viewport shows the promo bar, header, search, product image, thumbnails, title, color selector, and price, but the purchase button is not visible until the next scroll position. The button sits around 902 CSS px from the top on an 844 CSS px viewport.
findings.recommendation: Add a compact sticky bottom purchase bar that appears once the shopper scrolls past the product media. Include selected color, price, and "Add to Cart." Also reduce mobile header/search height or media height so the purchase path appears sooner.
findings.why_this_matters: Sticky mobile PDP CTAs have repeatedly lifted add-to-cart and completed-order metrics because the action remains available while shoppers evaluate content.
findings.callout_title: Mobile Add-to-Cart Is Below the First Viewport
findings.callout_body: Add a compact sticky bottom purchase bar that appears once the shopper scrolls past the product media. Include selected color, price, and "Add to Cart." Also reduce mobile header/search height or media height so the purchase path appears sooner.

findings.finding_title: CTA Says Select Color After Color Is Already Selected
findings.finding_body: The mobile page shows "Polished Tips" selected, but the primary CTA says "SELECT COLOR." This creates the impression that the page is blocked or the selected option was not accepted.
findings.observation: The mobile page shows "Polished Tips" selected, but the primary CTA says "SELECT COLOR." This creates the impression that the page is blocked or the selected option was not accepted.
findings.recommendation: When a valid variant is selected, change the CTA to "Add to Cart - $1,649.99" or "Add Polished Tips to Cart." If another required selection is missing, put the exact missing action above the button.
findings.why_this_matters: Specific action labels reduce uncertainty; generic or mismatched labels make shoppers spend effort interpreting the UI instead of buying.
findings.callout_title: CTA Says Select Color After Color Is Already Selected
findings.callout_body: When a valid variant is selected, change the CTA to "Add to Cart - $1,649.99" or "Add Polished Tips to Cart." If another required selection is missing, put the exact missing action above the button.

findings.finding_title: Header and Search Consume Too Much Mobile Buying Space
findings.finding_body: The mobile header plus promo/search area takes roughly the top 180 CSS px before the product content begins. That pushes title, price, and CTA lower and makes the first viewport feel like navigation rather than a product decision screen.
findings.observation: The mobile header plus promo/search area takes roughly the top 180 CSS px before the product content begins. That pushes title, price, and CTA lower and makes the first viewport feel like navigation rather than a product decision screen.
findings.recommendation: Collapse the search bar after initial load, make it an icon-triggered overlay, or reduce its vertical padding on PDPs. Preserve search access, but prioritize product title, price, and action.
findings.why_this_matters: Visual isolation and reduced competing chrome help the primary action area get seen sooner.
findings.callout_title: Header and Search Consume Too Much Mobile Buying Space
findings.callout_body: Collapse the search bar after initial load, make it an icon-triggered overlay, or reduce its vertical padding on PDPs. Preserve search access, but prioritize product title, price, and action.

findings.finding_title: High-Ticket Mobile PDP Has No Visible Reviews Before Purchase
findings.finding_body: The mobile purchase path shows title, color, price, BNPL, quantity, and CTA, but no star rating, review count, verified-buyer badge, or "read reviews" anchor before the shopper reaches the button.
findings.observation: The mobile purchase path shows title, color, price, BNPL, quantity, and CTA, but no star rating, review count, verified-buyer badge, or "read reviews" anchor before the shopper reaches the button.
findings.recommendation: Add a compact star/review row under the title. If product-specific reviews are unavailable, show honest category or brand review context separately and prioritize post-purchase review collection for this SKU.
findings.why_this_matters: Expensive products rely more heavily on social proof; review absence increases perceived purchase risk.
findings.callout_title: High-Ticket Mobile PDP Has No Visible Reviews Before Purchase
findings.callout_body: Add a compact star/review row under the title. If product-specific reviews are unavailable, show honest category or brand review context separately and prioritize post-purchase review collection for this SKU.

findings.finding_title: Fitment Assurance Is Not Tied to the Buy Button
findings.finding_body: The page eventually shows specifications for Make, Model, Year, Trim, and Part Number, but the mobile CTA area does not summarize fitment or inclusion confidence before purchase.
findings.observation: The page eventually shows specifications for Make, Model, Year, Trim, and Part Number, but the mobile CTA area does not summarize fitment or inclusion confidence before purchase.
findings.recommendation: Add a short trust strip above or below the CTA: "Fits 2016-2018 Focus RS," "Hardware + instructions included," "Secure checkout," and "Returns/warranty details." Link to the specification accordion.
findings.why_this_matters: Complex automotive products need enough compatibility detail on the PDP to prevent users from leaving to verify elsewhere.
findings.callout_title: Fitment Assurance Is Not Tied to the Buy Button
findings.callout_body: Add a short trust strip above or below the CTA: "Fits 2016-2018 Focus RS," "Hardware + instructions included," "Secure checkout," and "Returns/warranty details." Link to the specification accordion.

findings.finding_title: Free Shipping Eligibility Is Not Confirmed on the Product
findings.finding_body: The global promo says "FREE SHIPPING on most orders $75+ -- Contiguous US only," but the product area does not confirm whether this $1,649.99 exhaust qualifies or whether oversized shipping exceptions apply.
findings.observation: The global promo says "FREE SHIPPING on most orders $75+ -- Contiguous US only," but the product area does not confirm whether this $1,649.99 exhaust qualifies or whether oversized shipping exceptions apply.
findings.recommendation: Add a product-level line near the price: "Qualifies for free contiguous-US shipping" or "Oversized shipping calculated at checkout." The wording must reflect actual fulfillment rules.
findings.why_this_matters: Unexpected extra costs are a leading controllable cart-abandonment driver, especially when the item is physically large.
findings.callout_title: Free Shipping Eligibility Is Not Confirmed on the Product
findings.callout_body: Add a product-level line near the price: "Qualifies for free contiguous-US shipping" or "Oversized shipping calculated at checkout." The wording must reflect actual fulfillment rules.

findings.finding_title: BNPL Message Is Visible but Not Anchored to Value Confidence
findings.finding_body: The BNPL line appears before the CTA, but it does not sit beside shipping, return, warranty, or fitment confidence. On mobile it becomes a large line item without explaining why this $1,649 part is safe to finance.
findings.observation: The BNPL line appears before the CTA, but it does not sit beside shipping, return, warranty, or fitment confidence. On mobile it becomes a large line item without explaining why this $1,649 part is safe to finance.
findings.recommendation: Keep the installment price near the product price, but pair it with full-price clarity, shipping eligibility, and returns/warranty links. Do not let BNPL become the only confidence cue.
findings.why_this_matters: BNPL works best when installment awareness appears before purchase, but responsible display should preserve total-cost and trust clarity.
findings.callout_title: BNPL Message Is Visible but Not Anchored to Value Confidence
findings.callout_body: Keep the installment price near the product price, but pair it with full-price clarity, shipping eligibility, and returns/warranty links. Do not let BNPL become the only confidence cue.

findings.finding_title: Compare-At Price Lacks a Savings Explanation
findings.finding_body: Mobile shows the sale price and crossed-out regular price, but not the savings amount, percent, or anchor source. The shopper sees that the product is discounted but not why the anchor is credible.
findings.observation: Mobile shows the sale price and crossed-out regular price, but not the savings amount, percent, or anchor source. The shopper sees that the product is discounted but not why the anchor is credible.
findings.recommendation: Add "Save $198 (11%) vs. MSRP" if true, or label the anchor honestly as "Was." If the original price is not defensible, remove the crossed-out anchor.
findings.why_this_matters: Anchors influence value perception, but unexplained anchors can weaken trust when the product is expensive.
findings.callout_title: Compare-At Price Lacks a Savings Explanation
findings.callout_body: Add "Save $198 (11%) vs. MSRP" if true, or label the anchor honestly as "Was." If the original price is not defensible, remove the crossed-out anchor.

findings.finding_title: Mobile Product Title Is Not the Page H1
findings.finding_body: The captured DOM contains no H1. The visible product title is rendered as H2, even though it is the main product heading.
findings.observation: The captured DOM contains no H1. The visible product title is rendered as H2, even though it is the main product heading.
findings.recommendation: Render the product title as the single H1 for the PDP. Keep the same typography if desired; change the semantic element.
findings.why_this_matters: The main heading should clearly identify the product for users, assistive technology, and search engines.
findings.callout_title: Mobile Product Title Is Not the Page H1
findings.callout_body: Render the product title as the single H1 for the PDP. Keep the same typography if desired; change the semantic element.

findings.finding_title: Product Copy Has Visible Typos in the Purchase Path
findings.finding_body: The mobile PDP description includes errors such as "so would this would" and "thst." The copy also leads with repeated product phrasing before the actual buyer benefits.
findings.observation: The mobile PDP description includes errors such as "so would this would" and "thst." The copy also leads with repeated product phrasing before the actual buyer benefits.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite the first paragraph around the outcome: Borla ATAK sound, bolt-on Focus RS fitment, stainless construction, included hardware/instructions, and available tip finishes. Put detailed specs below that benefit-led opening.
findings.why_this_matters: Premium-product copy must build confidence quickly; typos and repetition reduce perceived brand value.
findings.callout_title: Product Copy Has Visible Typos in the Purchase Path
findings.callout_body: Rewrite the first paragraph around the outcome: Borla ATAK sound, bolt-on Focus RS fitment, stainless construction, included hardware/instructions, and available tip finishes. Put detailed specs below that benefit-led opening.

findings.finding_title: Product Schema Needs Merchant Listing Enhancements
findings.finding_body: The page has Product JSON-LD with offers, price, availability, SKU, and brand. It does not include aggregateRating/review data, shippingDetails, MerchantReturnPolicy, or ProductGroup variant structure for the two tip finishes.
findings.observation: The page has Product JSON-LD with offers, price, availability, SKU, and brand. It does not include aggregateRating/review data, shippingDetails, MerchantReturnPolicy, or ProductGroup variant structure for the two tip finishes.
findings.recommendation: Extend the JSON-LD with accurate reviews when available, shipping details, return policy, and ProductGroup/hasVariant markup for Polished Tips and Carbon Fiber Tips. Keep this synchronized with Shopify and Merchant Center.
findings.why_this_matters: Variant and merchant-listing metadata helps Google and AI shopping surfaces understand purchasable options, policies, and trust details.
findings.callout_title: Product Schema Needs Merchant Listing Enhancements
findings.callout_body: Extend the JSON-LD with accurate reviews when available, shipping details, return policy, and ProductGroup/hasVariant markup for Polished Tips and Carbon Fiber Tips. Keep this synchronized with Shopify and Merchant Center.
